120,Epic,Import Pipeline & Series Bible,"Deliver the Import Pipeline (FDX, Fountain, and PDF parsing orchestrated via Temporal sagas with confidence scoring and user review) and the Series Bible Service (Neo4j-backed character, location, fact, and world-rule graph with voice profiles and canon consistency checking). Epic 3 transforms ScriptOS from a greenfield authoring tool into a platform production teams can actually adopt: writers migrate existing scripts from Final Draft and Fountain without friction, and showrunners build out their series bible with character voices, world rules, and canon facts that feed AI grounding in Epic 4. The alpha milestone (Week 14, Sprint 6 end) depends on this epic being complete — the AI Service uses the Bible Service for context assembly. column1: 121,Feature,Import Pipeline,"The Import Service accepts FDX, Fountain, PDF, and scanned PDF files and converts them to the canonical Script AST via a five-stage pipeline: format detection → parser → normalization → confidence scoring → provenance record. The Import Saga (Temporal workflow) orchestrates the full lifecycle including a human-in-the-loop review step for low-confidence elements, then persists the AST to the Script Service and fires the NATS checkpointed event to trigger Elasticsearch indexing. Batch import handles zip archives of multiple episode files. Reference: Wiki/13-import-migration.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 4.","- Import Service auto-detects format from file extension and magic bytes

High-fidelity FDX round-trip is P0: writers who need to send scripts back to collaborators using Final Draft must not lose data. column1: 123,Task,Implement format detection module with magic-byte and extension-based classifier in the Import Service,"Create services/import/src/detection/format-detector.ts. Export detectFormat(file: { name: string
column2: buffer: Buffer }): ImportFormat enum { FDX, FOUNTAIN, PDF_DIGITAL, PDF_SCANNED, UNKNOWN }. Detection logic: (1) Extension check: .fdx → FDX
column4: .pdf → run PDF subtype check. (2) Magic bytes: PDF files start with %PDF-
column5: verify. (3) FDX magic: parse first 256 bytes as UTF-8
column6: check for <FinalDraft root element. (4) PDF subtype: use pdftotext (spawn child process) on the first page

column1: 124,Task,Implement FDX parser mapping Final Draft XML elements to all 19 AST node types,"Create services/import/src/parsers/fdx-parser.ts. Install: fast-xml-parser. Parser logic: (1) Parse FDX XML into DOM using fast-xml-parser with attribute handling. (2) Walk the FinalDraft > Content > Paragraph elements. (3) Map Paragraph Type attribute to AST node type: Scene Heading → scene_heading, Action → action, Character → character_name, Dialogue → dialogue, Parenthetical → parenthetical, Transition → transition, Shot → action (with metadata.isShot=true), General → general. (4) Extract SceneProperties for scene metadata: sceneNumber, sceneIntExt, sceneDay, sceneLocation. (5) Build parent/child hierarchy: each Scene Heading starts a new Scene node (UUIDv7)
column2: all following elements until the next Scene Heading become children. (6) Assign confidence: Paragraph elements with known Type → 0.99
column4: elements with empty text → 0.80. (7) Capture RevisionChange attributes per Paragraph for revision_color mapping (White, Blue, Pink, Yellow, Green, Goldenrod, Buff, Salmon, Cherry, Tan, Sage, Lilac, Double White). Integration test: parse 3 reference FDX files from services/import/test-fixtures/
column5: assert node counts, types, and scene structure.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 4,import-service
column8: sprint-4,,12,As a writer I can import existing FDX Fountain and PDF scripts into ScriptOS with automatic format detection and per-element confidence scoring

column1: 125,Task,Implement Fountain parser handling all Fountain spec element types including dual dialogue,"Create services/import/src/parsers/fountain-parser.ts. Implement the Fountain spec from fountain.io. Parsing rules: (1) Scene headings: lines starting with INT., EXT., INT./EXT., I/E, or a lone . (forced). Assign confidence 0.99 for INT./EXT. patterns
column2: 0.85 for forced headings. (2) Action: any block not matching other patterns. (3) Character: line in ALL CAPS (optionally with (V.O.), (O.S.), (O.C.) suffixes) immediately before dialogue. (4) Dialogue: block following a character line. (5) Parenthetical: line in (parentheses) between character and dialogue. (6) Transition: lines ending with TO: or starting with > (centered transitions). (7) Dual dialogue: ^ character marker triggers dual dialogue
column3: wrap both dialogue groups in dual_dialogue node. (8) Page breaks: === marks explicit page break. (9) Title page: key-value pairs before the first blank line + scene heading. Build scene tree by grouping elements from one scene heading to the next. Confidence scoring: well-formed patterns → 0.95
column4: ambiguous patterns → 0.72. Test against 3 reference Fountain files
column5: assert scene count, character count, and element type distribution.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 4,import-service
column8: sprint-4,,8,As a writer I can import existing FDX Fountain and PDF scripts into ScriptOS with automatic format detection and per-element confidence scoring

column1: 126,Task,Implement PDF text extraction with positional heuristics for element type classification,"Create services/import/src/parsers/pdf-parser.ts. Strategy: (1) Spawn pdftotext -layout -bbox child process to extract text with bounding-box coordinates per word. Parse output to get (text, x, y, width, height) per text block. (2) Group text blocks into lines by y-coordinate proximity (within 2pt). (3) Apply positional heuristics to classify each line: if x < 100px and text.startsWith('INT.') or 'EXT.' → scene_heading (confidence 0.97)
column2: if text === text.toUpperCase() and x between 200-280px → character_name (confidence 0.85)
column3: if x between 150-250px and line follows character → dialogue (confidence 0.82)
column6: if text matches (parenthetical) pattern → parenthetical (confidence 0.88). (4) Merge multi-line elements into single AST nodes. (5) Group by scene headings into scenes. For scanned PDFs: log warning and return SCANNED_PDF_REQUIRES_OCR error — OCR is a Phase 2 feature. Test against 2 digital PDF reference files
column10: sprint-4,,8,As a writer I can import existing FDX Fountain and PDF scripts into ScriptOS with automatic format detection and per-element confidence scoring

column1: 127,Task,Implement AST normalization layer and confidence scoring aggregation across all parsers,"Create services/import/src/normalization/normalizer.ts. This layer takes the raw parser output (parser-specific intermediate representation) and produces a uniform ASTNode array. Normalization steps: (1) Assign UUIDv7 IDs to all nodes (id = uuidv7()). (2) Build parent-child hierarchy: project → script → act (if explicit) → scene → element nodes
column2: assign position as fractional float64 index. (3) Normalize metadata: merge all parser-specific fields into the standard metadata jsonb structure per Wiki/03-data-model.md. (4) Confidence aggregation: ImportWarning[] = nodes where confidence < 0.95
column3: each warning has { nodeId, nodeType, confidence, reason }. (5) Overall confidence = weighted average of all node confidences (scene_heading and character_name weighted 2×
column4: action and dialogue weighted 1×). (6) Validate against ASTNode invariants from Wiki/03: every scene must have exactly one scene_heading as first child
column5: dialogue_group must have character_name. Fix violations automatically where possible
column6: flag unfixable as errors. Export normalize(rawNodes: RawNode[], format: ImportFormat): { nodes: ASTNode[]
column11: sprint-4,,8,As a writer I can import existing FDX Fountain and PDF scripts into ScriptOS with automatic format detection and per-element confidence scoring

column1: 129,User Story,As a developer the Import Saga orchestrates the full import lifecycle end-to-end via Temporal with human-in-the-loop review and batch zip support,"The Import Saga is a Temporal workflow that sequences all import pipeline steps reliably, handles failures with compensation, and pauses at the human review step for low-confidence imports. Temporal's durable execution guarantees that an import is never lost even if the worker pod restarts mid-import. The batch saga enables showrunners to import an entire season's worth of scripts from a zip file in a single operation with episode number auto-inference. Reference: Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md Temporal patterns, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 4.","- Import Saga workflow: detect format → create provenance + S3 archive → parse → normalize → if overallConfidence < 0.90: pause and signal UI for review → on review complete: persist AST to Script Service → trigger NATS script.checkpointed → mark provenance complete
- End-to-end import of a single FDX file completes in under 60s (excluding review wait time)
- Human review step: saga pauses at WaitForSignal activity
column2: writer sends reviewComplete signal with accepted/corrected element list

column1: - If worker pod restarts during any step: Temporal replays from last completed activity
column2: no data lost

column1: 130,Task,Implement Import Saga Temporal workflow with all 6 activities and compensation on failure,"Create workers/import-saga/src/workflows/import-saga.workflow.ts. Define workflow importScript(input: ImportWorkflowInput): Promise<ImportWorkflowResult>. Activity sequence (each with 3-retry policy, 30s start-to-close timeout): (1) createProvenanceActivity(file, orgId, scriptId) — S3 archive + DB record. (2) parseScriptActivity(provenanceId, format) — run format-specific parser
column2: return rawNodes + warnings. (3) normalizeASTActivity(rawNodes, format) — produce ASTNode[]
column3: return overallConfidence. (4) if overallConfidence < 0.90: waitForHumanReviewActivity — set provenance status='reviewing'
column5: wait for REVIEW_COMPLETE signal (timeout: 7 days) with accepted elements list. (5) persistASTActivity(scriptId, astNodes) — call Script Service UpsertASTNodes gRPC + CreateVersion gRPC. (6) completeProvenanceActivity(provenanceId, result) — update import_provenance status='complete', overallConfidence, completedAt. Compensation: if any activity fails, call markProvenanceFailedActivity. Deploy Temporal worker: workers/import-saga/src/index.ts connecting to Temporal Cloud namespace scriptos.{id}

column1: 133,Task,Implement revision color preservation from FDX RevisionChange attributes to AST node revision_color field,"Extend the FDX parser in services/import/src/parsers/fdx-parser.ts to capture revision change data. FDX encodes revisions as RevisionChange elements within Paragraph nodes (or as attributes on the Paragraph element depending on FDX version). Implementation: (1) During FDX XML walk, detect RevisionChange child elements on each Paragraph. (2) Map the FDX revision name to the RevisionColor enum in packages/ast: White → WHITE, Blue → BLUE, Pink → PINK, Yellow → YELLOW, Green → GREEN, Goldenrod → GOLDENROD, Buff → BUFF, Salmon → SALMON, Cherry → CHERRY, Tan → TAN, Sage → SAGE, Lilac → LILAC, Double White → DOUBLE_WHITE. (3) Set revision_color field on the corresponding ASTNode. (4) If a Paragraph has no RevisionChange: set revision_color = WHITE (default). Integration test: parse a test FDX file with known revision marks
column2: assert all 3 colored pages preserved with correct RevisionColor values on the ASTNode.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 4,import-service

column1: 140,Feature,Series Bible Service,"The Series Bible Service manages a Neo4j Aura graph of characters, locations, props, world rules, story arcs, and lore facts. Every entity has a stable UUID, is project-scoped, and participates in a rich relationship graph (APPEARS_IN, SET_IN, HAS_VOICE, SUPERSEDES, RELATED_TO, PARTICIPATES_IN). The canon consistency checker uses graph traversal to detect contradictions between new facts and established ones. Voice profiles per character capture vocabulary, speech patterns, and forbidden words for AI grounding. Stub entities are auto-created when character and location names are detected on script checkpoint. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md Series Bible Graph, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 5.","- Character, Location, Fact, Rule, VoiceProfile nodes created and queryable in Neo4j Aura
- APPEARS_IN and SET_IN relationships created when character/location first linked to a script scene
- Fact supersedure chain traversable: SUPERSEDES relationship queryable via Cypher path query
- Canon consistency check: validateScriptConsistency gRPC returns ConflictResult[] for a contradictory test case
- Voice profile stored with vocabularyLevel, speechPatterns, forbiddenWords, referenceSceneIds
- AI context gRPC methods (getWorldRules, getCharacterFacts, getVoiceProfiles) all returning correct data
- Character and location stub nodes auto-created from script.checkpointed NATS events within 30s
- Bible Service deployed to staging with Neo4j Aura connection
column2: /health returns 200",1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service

column1: 141,User Story,As a writer I can create and manage characters locations facts and world rules in the Series Bible,"The Series Bible is where showrunners and writers capture the living canon of their story: who the characters are, where scenes take place, what rules govern the story world, and what facts have been established across episodes. These entities are linked back to specific script scenes so writers can trace where any fact originated. All CRUD operations must be project-scoped and support soft deactivation so facts are never lost but can be superseded. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md Node Labels and Property Schemas, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 5.","- Character node: create with { name, description, characterType ('protagonist','antagonist','supporting','minor') }

column1: 142,Task,Apply Neo4j Cypher constraints and vector index for the Series Bible Graph schema in packages/db,"Create packages/db/migrations/bible/constraints.cypher with all Cypher DDL from Wiki/03-data-model.md Cypher Constraints section. Uniqueness constraints: CREATE CONSTRAINT char_id FOR (n:Character) REQUIRE n.id IS UNIQUE
column2: location_id, fact_id, rule_id, arc_id, lore_id, voice_id, scene_ref_id, ep_ref_id. Existence constraints on mandatory properties: REQUIRE n.orgId IS NOT NULL on Character, Location, Fact, Rule. Vector index: CALL db.index.vector.createNodeIndex('factEmbeddings', 'Fact', 'embedding', 1536, 'cosine') for OpenAI text-embedding-3-large dimension. Composite index for project queries: CREATE INDEX char_project FOR (n:Character) ON (n.orgId, n.projectId)
column3: same for Location, Fact, Rule. Create a migration runner script (packages/db/scripts/apply-bible-constraints.ts) that connects to Neo4j Aura and applies all constraints idempotently (SHOW CONSTRAINTS → skip if exists). column2: GetCharacter(GetCharacterRequest) → CharacterResponse
column5: LinkCharacterToScene(characterId, sceneRefId, role) → creates APPEARS_IN relationship. Cypher for create: CREATE (c:Character { id: $id, name: $name, description: $desc, characterType: $type, orgId: $orgId, projectId: $projectId, isActive: true, createdAt: datetime(), updatedAt: datetime() }). Cypher for APPEARS_IN: MATCH (c:Character {id: $cId}), (s:SceneRef {id: $sId}) CREATE (c)-[:APPEARS_IN { role: $role, linkedAt: datetime() }]->(s). SceneRef nodes created on first reference (MERGE pattern). Integration test: createCharacter → linkToScene → getCharacter → assert character has APPEARS_IN to scene. Write unit tests for all Cypher queries using neo4j-driver TestContainers or a mock driver.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service

column1: 144,Task,Implement Bible Service location CRUD with SET_IN scene linkage in Neo4j,"Implement services/bible/src/handlers/location.handler.ts. gRPC: CreateLocation, GetLocation, UpdateLocation, DeactivateLocation, LinkLocationToScene. Cypher patterns mirror Character handler with Location label and SET_IN relationship. Additional location properties: intExt (extracted from scene headings: INT.=interior, EXT.=exterior, INT./EXT.=both), linkedSceneCount (computed via COUNT query for display). Cypher for SET_IN: MATCH (l:Location {id: $lId}), (s:SceneRef {id: $sId}) MERGE (l)-[:SET_IN { linkedAt: datetime() }]->(s). GetLocation returns: { id, name, locationType, description, linkedSceneCount, linkedEpisodes, isActive }. Integration test: createLocation → linkToScene via SET_IN → getLocation → assert linkedSceneCount = 1.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service

column1: 145,Task,Implement Bible Service fact creation with provenance chain and SUPERSEDES relationship in Neo4j,"Implement services/bible/src/handlers/fact.handler.ts. A fact captures a canonical truth about the story world anchored to the scene where it was established. Cypher for create: CREATE (f:Fact { id: $id, factText: $text, confidence: $confidence, tags: $tags, sourceSceneId: $sourceSceneId, orgId: $orgId, projectId: $projectId, isActive: true, createdAt: datetime() }). On superseding an existing fact: MATCH (old:Fact {id: $oldId}) CREATE (new:Fact {...}) CREATE (new)-[:SUPERSEDES { reason: $reason, supersededAt: datetime() }]->(old) SET old.isActive = false. Supersedure chain query: MATCH path = (f:Fact {id: $id})-[:SUPERSEDES*0..10]->(ancestor) RETURN path — traversable for audit. Embedding: on create, call OpenAI text-embedding-3-large API with factText
column2: store as embedding property (float[] 1536 dims) for semantic search in Sprint 6. Integration test: createFact → supersedeFact → query chain → assert chain length = 2 → assert old fact isActive=false.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service

column1: 147,User Story,As a service consumer the Bible Service provides gRPC APIs for canon consistency checking AI context assembly and character voice profiles,"The AI Service in Sprint 6 will call the Bible Service to assemble context for dialogue suggestions and consistency checks. The Bible Service must expose reliable gRPC batch methods that return world rules, character facts, and voice profiles for a given project and character set. The canon consistency checker detects contradictions between new writer-asserted facts and established bible facts using graph traversal — it is the core of the AI-assisted canon guardian feature. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md Conflict Detection Algorithm, Wiki/05-ai-governance.md Context Assembly.","- validateScriptConsistency(projectId, newFacts[]) gRPC: detects contradictions
column2: returns ConflictResult[] with { existingFactId, newFactId, conflictType, severity: 'ERROR' | 'WARNING' | 'INFO' }

column1: 148,Task,Implement validateScriptConsistency gRPC for canon conflict detection using Neo4j graph traversal,"Implement services/bible/src/handlers/consistency.handler.ts. Input: ValidateScriptConsistencyRequest { projectId, newFacts: [{ id, factText, confidence, tags[], sourceSceneId }] }. For each new fact: (1) Generate embedding via OpenAI text-embedding-3-large API. (2) Semantic search in Neo4j vector index: CALL db.index.vector.queryNodes('factEmbeddings', 10, $embedding) YIELD node, score WHERE node.projectId = $projectId AND node.isActive = true RETURN node, score. (3) For each result with score > 0.88 (high semantic similarity): run contradiction heuristic — if factText contains negation keywords (not, never, no longer, opposite) compared to similar established fact → classify as CONTRADICTION (severity ERROR)
column2: if partially overlapping claims → WARNING. (4) Also run rule-based checks: if new fact asserts a character attribute that contradicts an existing character attribute fact for same character + same attribute type → ERROR. Return ConflictResult[]. Integration test: establish fact 'Walter is a high school teacher' → call validate with new fact 'Walter is a drug lord' → assert ConflictResult returned with severity ERROR.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service

column1: 149,Task,Implement character voice profile CRUD with HAS_VOICE relationship in Neo4j,"Implement services/bible/src/handlers/voice-profile.handler.ts. Each character has at most one VoiceProfile (one-to-one via HAS_VOICE relationship). gRPC: CreateVoiceProfile({ characterId, vocabularyLevel, speechPatterns[], forbiddenWords[], referenceSceneIds[] }) → VoiceProfileResponse. If character already has a profile: return ALREADY_EXISTS with profile id. UpdateVoiceProfile: MATCH (v:VoiceProfile { id: $id }) SET v.vocabularyLevel = $level, v.speechPatterns = $patterns, v.forbiddenWords = $words, v.updatedAt = datetime(). GetVoiceProfile(characterId): MATCH (c:Character {id: $cId})-[:HAS_VOICE]->(v:VoiceProfile) RETURN v. AddReferenceScene(profileId, sceneId): appends sceneId to v.referenceSceneIds (array property)
column2: max 5 reference scenes enforced (return FAILED_PRECONDITION if already at 5). Integration test: create character → create voice profile → add 3 reference scenes → getVoiceProfile → assert all fields correct → attempt to add 6th scene → assert FAILED_PRECONDITION error.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service
column6: sprint-5,,6,As a service consumer the Bible Service provides gRPC APIs for canon consistency checking AI context assembly and character voice profiles

column1: 150,Task,Implement getWorldRules getCharacterFacts and getVoiceProfiles batch gRPC methods for AI context assembly,"Implement services/bible/src/handlers/ai-context.handler.ts. Three gRPC methods optimised for AI context assembly (called by AI Service in Sprint 6 in the hot path for dialogue suggestions): (1) GetWorldRules(projectId, category?): MATCH (r:Rule { orgId: $orgId, projectId: $projectId, isActive: true }) RETURN r ORDER BY r.category, r.createdAt
column2: return all rules (typically 5-20 for a series). (2) GetCharacterFacts(characterId, limit=20): MATCH (f:Fact)-[:ABOUT]->(c:Character {id: $cId}) WHERE f.isActive = true RETURN f ORDER BY f.confidence DESC LIMIT $limit. (3) GetVoiceProfiles(characterIds[]): batch lookup of voice profiles for up to 10 characters in a single Cypher query: MATCH (c:Character)-[:HAS_VOICE]->(v:VoiceProfile) WHERE c.id IN $ids RETURN c.id, v. All methods must complete in under 100ms for typical project sizes (benchmarked in integration tests). These are read-only

column1: 152,Task,Implement NATS consumer in Script Service that extracts character and location names from checkpointed scenes and creates Bible stubs,"Implement services/script/src/consumers/bible-stub.consumer.ts. Subscribe to NATS subject scriptos.script.checkpointed (durable consumer: bible-stub). On each event: (1) Fetch the changed scene IDs from the event payload. (2) Call Script Service GetSceneSnapshot gRPC for each changed scene to retrieve headingText and raw element text. (3) Extract character names: collect all character_name node textContent values from the scene's AST nodes. (4) Extract location names: parse scene_heading text — split on INT./EXT., extract the location portion (before the dash). (5) For each extracted name: call Bible Service GetCharacterByName(name, projectId) gRPC — if NOT_FOUND: call Bible Service CreateCharacter with isStub=true, name=extractedName. Same pattern for Location. (6) isStub=true marks auto-created entries so writers know they need enrichment. Processing latency target: all stubs created within 30s of checkpoint event. Integration test: checkpoint event with 2 character names → verify 2 Character stub nodes created in Neo4j.",,1,New,ScriptOS,ScriptOS\Sprint 5,bible-service
